---
title: "Gibbs Sampling for Bayesian Gaussian Mixture Models"
author: "PhD Applicant"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

## Overview

This document implements Gibbs Sampling, a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) technique, to perform fully Bayesian inference on a Gaussian Mixture Model. It estimates the full posterior distribution of the component means, precisions, and mixing weights.

## Algorithm Implementation

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
set.seed(42)
```

```{r gibbs-functions}
# Helper function to sample from a Dirichlet distribution
rdirichlet <- function(alpha) {
  x <- rgamma(length(alpha), shape = alpha, rate = 1)
  return(x / sum(x))
}

gibbs_sampling_gmm <- function(X, K, num_samples = 5000, burn_in = 1000) {
  n <- length(X)
  
  # Hyperparameters for Priors
  alpha_prior <- rep(1, K)
  mu_0 <- 0.0
  tau_0 <- 1.0
  shape_0 <- 0.1
  rate_0 <- 0.1
  
  # Initialization
  pi_k <- rep(1/K, K)
  mu <- sample(X, K, replace = FALSE)
  tau <- rep(1, K)
  
  # Storage
  samples_mu <- matrix(0, nrow = num_samples, ncol = K)
  samples_pi <- matrix(0, nrow = num_samples, ncol = K)
  
  for (it in 1:(num_samples + burn_in)) {
    # 1. Sample Latent Variables (Z)
    probs <- matrix(0, nrow = n, ncol = K)
    for (k in 1:K) {
      probs[, k] <- pi_k[k] * sqrt(tau[k]) * exp(-0.5 * tau[k] * (X - mu[k])^2)
    }
    probs <- probs / rowSums(probs)
    
    # Multinomial sampling for each observation
    Z <- apply(probs, 1, function(p) sample(1:K, size = 1, prob = p))
    
    # Summary statistics
    N_k <- sapply(1:K, function(k) sum(Z == k))
    sum_x <- sapply(1:K, function(k) sum(X[Z == k]))
    
    # 2. Sample Mixing Probabilities (pi)
    pi_k <- rdirichlet(alpha_prior + N_k)
    
    # 3. Sample Means (mu)
    for (k in 1:K) {
      tau_n <- tau_0 + N_k[k] * tau[k]
      mu_n <- (tau_0 * mu_0 + tau[k] * sum_x[k]) / tau_n
      mu[k] <- rnorm(1, mean = mu_n, sd = 1/sqrt(tau_n))
    }
    
    # 4. Sample Precisions (tau)
    for (k in 1:K) {
      shape_n <- shape_0 + N_k[k] / 2.0
      rate_n <- rate_0 + 0.5 * sum((X[Z == k] - mu[k])^2)
      tau[k] <- rgamma(1, shape = shape_n, rate = rate_n + 1e-10)
    }
    
    # Store samples
    if (it > burn_in) {
      samples_mu[it - burn_in, ] <- mu
      samples_pi[it - burn_in, ] <- pi_k
    }
  }
  
  return(list(mu = samples_mu, pi = samples_pi))
}
```

## Example Usage

```{r run-gibbs}
# Generate synthetic bimodal data
data <- c(rnorm(100, mean = -3, sd = 1), rnorm(100, mean = 4, sd = 1.5))

# Run MCMC
post_samples <- gibbs_sampling_gmm(data, K = 2, num_samples = 2000, burn_in = 500)

cat("Posterior Mean of Mu:", colMeans(post_samples$mu), "\n")
cat("Posterior Mean of Pi:", colMeans(post_samples$pi), "\n")
```
